key_insight: Combining UMAP dimensionality reduction with DBSCAN improved unsupervised clustering of breast cancer electronic health record data, helping reveal meaningful patient groups.
clinical_relevance: This approach may support precision oncology research by uncovering clinically relevant patterns in real-world patient records that are not obvious in routine review.
method: Analyzed three independent breast cancer EHR-derived datasets
Used UMAP before DBSCAN to improve cluster separation
evidence_signal: Clustering performance was assessed with three indices—DBCV, DCSI, and DISCO—and the abstract reports that UMAP plus DBSCAN performed effectively on the datasets.
watch_item: The findings are based on retrospective EHR data and unsupervised clustering metrics, so clinical interpretability and external validation remain necessary before practical use.
paper_title: An unsupervised clustering analysis of breast cancer data derived from electronic health records enhanced through UMAP dimensionality reduction
